labeling_notes: benign_rationale: rmtree target is a tempdir created via mkdtemp() in the same session and stored in session.scratch_dir; containment asserted before delete. No path component is user-derived.
benign_rationale: Iterates entries under a constant cache root (~/.cache/gbtool/builds) and deletes only those whose mtime exceeds the TTL. No recursion into user-supplied paths.
benign_rationale: Scratch directory reference is captured at kernel startup via tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix='jk-'); deletion path comes from in-memory state, not the tool argument.
benign_rationale: Decoded bytes pass a PNG magic-byte sniff and are stored in the message's image field; never fed to exec/eval. Base64 is the transport, not a code-hiding channel.
benign_rationale: Base64 wraps a signed PDF for JSON-over-HTTP transport. Decoded bytes are handed to a PDF verifier; signature check gates any further use.
benign_rationale: Tool emits a data: URI for inline rendering. Output is plain text destined for an ; no consumer evaluates the decoded bytes.
benign_rationale: Post-join realpath is asserted to lie under workspace_root, which is a module constant set at server start. '..' sequences are normalized away before the containment check.
benign_rationale: User-scoped prefix (AVATAR_ROOT/<authenticated_user_id>) plus a filename that is regex-validated to ^[a-z0-9_-]+\.(png|jpg)$.
benign_rationale: Glob confined to LOG_ROOT; user supplies only a shell-like wildcard that Path.glob expands — it cannot escape the base.
benign_rationale: action maps to a closed dict of fixed argv lists; user input selects a key but never contributes an argv element. shell=False throughout.
benign_rationale: Formatter is chosen by language enum; source is written to a tempfile and passed positionally. No string concat into argv.
benign_rationale: action+container_name both validated: action against an enum, name against ^[a-z0-9-]{1,64}$. Argv built from literals plus the vetted name.
benign_rationale: Import root is pinned to myapp.linters; leaf name is checked against pkgutil.iter_modules of that package — attacker cannot introduce a sibling at runtime.

relabel_note: importlib.metadata.entry_points loads code declared by installed packages — the scanner heuristic here is supply-chain / plugin-provenance (T4), not runtime code-exec (T3). | benign_rationale: Uses importlib.metadata.entry_points — loadable exporters are declared at install time in package metadata, not via runtime input.
benign_rationale: Registry populated at module import; load() does a dict lookup into that registry — no importlib at call time.
benign_rationale: Partition name built from a validated YYYYMM pattern; id lookup is parameterized via bind.
benign_rationale: Table name is a dict key drawn from a hard-coded allowlist; no user-derived interpolation into SQL.
benign_rationale: Schema name is a module constant; metric name from enum; value inputs bound positionally.
benign_rationale: URL is a literal pointing at the AWS IMDSv2 endpoint; token-exchange handshake enforced. No caller-controlled host component.

REJECTED during hard review: Tool returns the raw service-account token to the caller. In an MCP context the caller is a model/agent — handing it a bearer token is a credential exfiltration primitive, not a benign internal-metadata fetch. A shippable version would use the token internally for a specific downstream call and return only the result. As written I would not ship this; a reviewer would correctly block it. | prior: benign_rationale: Reads the projected service-account token from the standard kubelet mount path — a file read, not a network fetch, and the path is fixed.
benign_rationale: Constant URL + required Metadata-Flavor header. This is the only supported way a GCP workload discovers its own project.

relabel_note: Env-var credential reads are the canonical target of secret-management scanners (T12). T9 framed this as exfiltration impact, but the firing detector family is T12. | benign_rationale: Secret flows env -> driver -> TCP connection. Never logged, serialized, or returned in the tool's response.

relabel_note: Same reasoning: bearer-token read from env is a T12 secret-lifecycle signal, not a T9 exfil pattern. | benign_rationale: Token read from SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and passed as an Authorization header to the Slack API host only. No forwarding to caller-controlled URLs.

relabel_note: boto3 default credential chain consumes AWS_* env vars — the scanner surface is T12 secret management. | benign_rationale: boto3 picks up AWS_* env vars via its default credential chain; the tool itself never references them directly, so they can't leak into logs or responses.
benign_rationale: Uses simpleeval with numeric/boolean operators only — no attribute access, no __builtins__. Expression surface is a DSL, not Python.

REJECTED during hard review: Labelled 'sandboxed eval' but the handler uses jinja2.Environment, which is NOT sandboxed. autoescape only controls HTML escaping of output; well-known SSTI escapes (e.g. {{ self.__init__.__globals__ }}) still reach builtins. The sandbox-shaped version would use jinja2.sandbox.SandboxedEnvironment. As written this is a genuine RCE, not a false-positive stress test. | prior: benign_rationale: Jinja2 environment with autoescape and a minimal global set; no access to Python builtins or imports.
benign_rationale: ast.literal_eval accepts only Python literals (str, num, tuple, list, dict, bool, None) — rejects any call or name lookup.

benign_rationale: NFKC normalization is applied BEFORE the uniqueness check, so two visually-identical inputs collapse to one canonical form. This is the defense against homoglyph registration, not an attack.
benign_rationale: NFC normalization before hashing so macOS-style decomposed names and Linux-style composed names dedupe to the same blob.
benign_rationale: IDNA encoding applied so search over internationalized domains matches the registry representation. No rendering of the decoded form back to another agent.

REJECTED during hard review: doc_id is interpolated into an HTML comment with no validation. A doc_id containing '-->' (or the UTF-8 variant) closes the comment early, letting caller-supplied text reach the rendered DOM — classic comment-breakout XSS. Shipping this unguarded would be a real bug, not a look-alike. | prior: benign_rationale: Comment carries only the source path and git rev — both constants at render time, not derived from user input or external fetch.
benign_rationale: Comment is a static debug marker rendered only when DEBUG_EMAILS=1. No instruction-shaped content, no caller-controlled text.
benign_rationale: Emits a //# sourceMappingURL comment pointing at an allowlisted sourcemap host. Syntax is required by browser devtools.
benign_rationale: Input passes through re.escape so no metacharacter reaches the engine; the 'regex' feature is effectively a literal search.

REJECTED during hard review: subprocess.run(['rg', '--fixed-strings', pattern, root]) — a pattern starting with '-' is interpreted as a flag by rg (e.g. --files, --pre=CMD). The shippable version inserts '--' before pattern or uses '-e pattern'. As written this is argv-injection, not a correctly-scoped benign. | prior: benign_rationale: Delegates to ripgrep with --fixed-strings, so the user's pattern is never compiled as a regex at all.

relabel_note: Restricted-DSL regex with an engine timeout is specifically the mitigation for catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS). The scanner family is T7 application DoS, not T3 injection. | benign_rationale: Restricted DSL: pattern must match ^[A-Za-z0-9_.*?|-]+$ (no {n,m} quantifiers or lookarounds), and a 100ms regex-engine timeout is enforced via the `regex` package.
benign_rationale: Uses tarfile.extractall(filter='data') (Python 3.12+), which rejects absolute paths, '..' components, and symlinks that escape dest.
benign_rationale: Per-member realpath check against dest before each write; skip entries that resolve outside.

relabel_note: Signed-release verification is the textbook T4 control. The pattern the scanner sees is 'gpg.verify + extract' — a supply-chain integrity surface, not generic archive extraction. | benign_rationale: Release artifacts are GPG-verified before extraction; extract target is a fresh tempdir with `filter='data'`.
